description: Identifies and modifies negative thought patterns and behaviors. Focuses on the relationship between thoughts, feelings, and actions.
Gradual exposure to feared situations without performing compulsive behaviors. Reduces anxiety through habituation.
Skills-based therapy focusing on acceptance and change. Includes mindfulness, distress tolerance, emotion regulation, interpersonal effectiveness.
resources_needed: DBT-trained therapist, skills groups, phone coaching

description: Focuses on improving relationships and social functioning. Addresses grief, role disputes, transitions, and interpersonal deficits.
Emphasizes accepting thoughts/feelings while pursuing meaningful life values. Combines mindfulness with behavioral change.
Training in mindfulness meditation to reduce stress and improve well-being. Combines meditation with yoga.
resources_needed: MBSR instructor, meditation cushion, quiet space

description: Controlled breathing techniques (4-7-8, box breathing) to calm nervous system. Can be done anytime, anywhere.
Sequential tensing and relaxing of muscle groups to reduce physical tension and anxiety.
Regular exercise (150 min/week) promotes endorphin release and improves mood. Also improves sleep and self-esteem.
effectiveness: High (as effective as medication for mild depression)

description: Establishing consistent sleep schedule, limiting screen time, creating dark/cool bedroom. Crucial for mental health.
Writing about feelings and experiences to process emotions and identify patterns. Improves emotional regulation.
Regularly acknowledging positive aspects of life. Shifts focus from negative to positive. Can include gratitude journaling.
Maintaining strong relationships, joining groups, volunteering. One of strongest protective factors for mental health.
Balanced diet with omega-3s, vitamins, minerals. Avoids excessive sugar/caffeine. Food directly affects brain chemistry.
Uses bilateral stimulation (eye movements) while recalling trauma to process and integrate traumatic memories.
Learning about mental health condition, symptoms, treatment options. Reduces depression and increases treatment compliance.
Learning to express needs clearly and respectfully. Improves relationships and self-esteem.
Breaking large goals into manageable steps. Increases sense of accomplishment and reduces overwhelm.
Structured approach to identifying problems and generating solutions. Reduces sense of helplessness.
Engaging in activities that produce laughter. Reduces stress hormones and promotes well-being.
